title: Can AI tell if your food is ultra-processed? CMC Vellore doctor breaks down what studies found
description: Artificial intelligence is now helping identify ultra-processed foods. A recent study shows tools like ChatGPT can classify foods with high accuracy. This capability is crucial as ultra-processed foods link to serious health issues. AI can speed up dietary analysis for researchers. Experts will still provide oversight. This technology offers a fast way to understand food processing in modern diets.

title: Specsavers co-founder funds PhD eye research
description: NEW research, including the use of artificial intelligence to assess the progression of a common form of blindness, is being funded by Specsavers co-founder Dame Mary Perkins.
content: The research – a four-year project – is being undertaken by PhD student Maria Patsiamanidi, who will use AI and machine learning to analyse thousands of retinal images to better understand how age-related macular degeneration progresses from its earl... [1572 chars]

title: TeleCanor Global Launches iQIS AI Platform and Announces Participation in Future Alpha 2026 Expo
description: TeleCanor Global Limited has launched iQIS (Intelligent Quantitative Investment Systems), an AI-driven platform for quantitative investment research and index design, accessible at https://iqis.us/. The platform targets financial researchers, asset managers, and institutions with advanced analytics and AI tools for financial data analysis and investment model building. The company will participate in Future Alpha 2026 – Quant + Alpha Innovation Expo in New York, showcasing modules including AI Index Foundry, AI Powered Factor Index Studio, QIS Risk and Fragility Scanner, and Quant Strategy Operating System. This development represents the company's strategic expansion into financial technology, artificial intelligence, and data analytics space.
content: TeleCanor Global Limited has announced significant developments in its technology portfolio with the launch of an artificial intelligence-driven investment platform and participation in a major international fintech expo. The company disclosed these ... [6752 chars]

title: Wednesday briefing: From missing billions to nonexistent datacentres, inside Britain’s AI drive

title: Rupee tailwind vs AI scare: Are India's beaten-down IT stocks attractive enough to buy amid US-Iran war?
description: Indian IT stocks are facing a dual challenge. Fears of artificial intelligence disrupting traditional outsourcing models are weighing on the sector. However, geopolitical tensions and a weaker rupee are providing unexpected support. Analysts are divided on whether current valuations offer attractive entry points for long-term investors. The sector is at a crossroads, with a structural transition underway.
content: Indian IT stocks, battered by a sharp selloff over the past two months amid fears that artificial intelligence could disrupt the traditional outsourcing model, may be finding unexpected support from geopolitical turmoil. The escalation of tensions be... [8957 chars]
